name: Colmenar del Arroyo and Sevilla la Nueva Bunkers Loop
name_original: Sevilla la Nueva-Colmenar del Arroyo – circular visitando búnkeres de la Guerra Civil
description: This difficult 38.1-mile mountain bike route near Sevilla la Nueva explores Spanish Civil War bunkers and offers challenging terrain.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Sevilla La Nueva offer varied terrain suitable for different skill levels, situated within the Community of Madrid. The area features protected natural spaces, including the Parque Regional del Curso Medio del Río Guadarrama, characterized by indigenous holm oak groves. Riders can expect mixed surfaces, from tracks and paths to sections with elevation changes. This region provides a diverse landscape for mountain biking, combining natural beauty with accessible trail networks. Best mountain bike trails around Sevilla La Nueva The most popular mountain bike trail is Colmenar del Arroyo and Sevilla la Nueva Bunkers Loop, a 38.1 miles (61.4 km) difficult trail that takes 5 hours 11 minutes to complete. This route features historical Spanish Civil War bunkers and significant elevation gains. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Fuente del Testero – Villamantilla Town Center loop from Sevilla la Nueva, a moderate 23.2 miles (37.4 km) path. This trail offers mixed surfaces and requires good fitness for some sections. Local mountain bikers also love the Open Fields Near Brunete – View Under the Concrete Bridge loop from Sevilla la Nueva, a 13.6 miles (21.9 km) trail leading through open fields, often completed in about 1 hour 48 minutes. Mountain biking around Sevilla La Nueva is defined by varied terrain, holm oak groves, and historical elements. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to challenging routes. The routes in Sevilla La Nueva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 3000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Sevilla La Nueva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Sevilla La Nueva?
A: The mountain bike trails around Sevilla La Nueva offer a diverse range of terrain, from easy paths to challenging routes with significant elevation changes. You'll encounter mixed surfaces, including tracks and paths, often within indigenous holm oak groves and open fields. Some sections may require advanced riding skills or even pushing your bike on steeper climbs.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Sevilla La Nueva?
A: Yes, Sevilla La Nueva offers several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40388589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Open Fields Near Brunete – View Under the Concrete Bridge loop from Sevilla la Nueva&lt;/a&gt; is an easier route, suitable for any fitness level and skill. Overall, about half of the 14 available routes are considered easy.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes in the area?
A: For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961624041/colmenar-del-arroyo-and-sevilla-la-nueva-bunkers-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colmenar del Arroyo and Sevilla la Nueva Bunkers Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 38.1-mile route featuring historical Spanish Civil War bunkers and significant elevation gains of up to 3,000 feet.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails in natural areas like the Parque Regional del Curso Medio del Río Guadarrama, which encompasses Sevilla La Nueva, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife, and to check specific local regulations for any particular trail or park section you plan to visit.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or interesting sights along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, some mountain bike routes incorporate historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961624041/colmenar-del-arroyo-and-sevilla-la-nueva-bunkers-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colmenar del Arroyo and Sevilla la Nueva Bunkers Loop&lt;/a&gt; passes by Spanish Civil War bunkers. Additionally, you might encounter other points of interest like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1629985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aulencia Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19130873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aulencia Castle – Open Fields Near Brunete loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Sevilla La Nueva?
A: The region around Sevilla La Nueva is suitable for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are advisable. Winter rides are also possible, though some trails might be muddy after rain.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Sevilla La Nueva are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38220851" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Testero – Villamantilla Town Center loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40387219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;House of the Fairies – View Under the Concrete Bridge loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features define the landscape for mountain biking in this area?
A: The landscape around Sevilla La Nueva is characterized by its location within the Parque Regional del Curso Medio del Río Guadarrama, featuring indigenous holm oak groves, open fields, and varied terrain. It's part of a significant green corridor in the Region of Madrid, offering diverse ecosystems and opportunities to observe local flora and fauna.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Sevilla La Nueva?
A: The mountain bike trails in Sevilla La Nueva are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 3,000 mountain bikers have explored the varied terrain, often praising the diverse routes and natural beauty of the area.

Q: Are there any other attractions or points of interest near the mountain bike trails?
A: Beyond the trails, the area offers several points of interest. You can visit historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2675439" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalcarnero Main Square&lt;/a&gt; or explore various bridges such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1394042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Bridge of Móstoles&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1393760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wooden Bridge over the Guadarrama River in Boadilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Sevilla La Nueva?
A: There are 14 mountain bike trails available around Sevilla La Nueva, offering a range of difficulties from easy to difficult. This includes 7 easy, 5 moderate, and 2 difficult routes.
tips: Panoramic point with beautiful views of several of the highest peaks in Madrid, including Cabeza Gordo and Peña de Cenihundos. It is reached by a good gravel forest track, ideal for warming up and gaining meters!
The area of El Espino and Pico del Pañuelo up to Colmenar del Arroyo is full of old bunkers that were used during the Civil War.
Former war bunker in excellent condition.

The bridge over the Perales River offers beautiful views from where you can take a panoramic view and take the opportunity to catch your breath, and in summer you can go down to the riverbed and take a refreshing swim.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This is a challenging route that generally takes around 5 hours and 11 minutes to complete. However, given its difficulty and varied terrain, it's wise to plan for potentially longer, especially if you stop to explore the historical sites.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route features a highly varied and often demanding terrain. You'll encounter approximately 25.1 km of loose ground and 23.4 km of natural or unpaved paths. There are also about 6.30 km of paved sections and 883 meters of singletrack, requiring advanced riding skills and good bike handling.

faqs.question: What historical sites or viewpoints can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in historical and scenic points of interest. You'll have the opportunity to visit several Spanish Civil War bunkers, including the well-preserved Spanish Civil War Bunkers near Colmenar del Arroyo and specifically Bunker 13. Additionally, you can enjoy views from the Viewpoint of Cabeza Gordo and Peña de Cenicientos and cross the Bridge Over the Perales River.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as a 'difficult' mountain bike ride. It requires very good fitness and advanced riding skills. Some sections may even necessitate pushing your bike. It is not recommended for beginners or families with young children.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: To avoid extreme temperatures, the best times to ride this route are typically spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). Summers can be very hot, and winters can bring cold and potentially wet conditions, making the already challenging terrain more difficult.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sevilla la Nueva-Colmenar del Arroyo mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: As a circular route starting and ending in Sevilla la Nueva, you can typically find parking within the town itself. Alternatively, if you prefer to start closer to the bunkers, parking options may be available in Colmenar del Arroyo.

faqs.question: Is the Sevilla la Nueva-Colmenar del Arroyo mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on public trails in Spain, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially given the length and difficulty of this route, as well as the presence of varied terrain and potential wildlife. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a long, challenging ride.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, no special permits are required for mountain biking on public trails in this region of Madrid. However, always respect local regulations and private property signs you might encounter along the way.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notable sections include parts of the Ruta Verde 5.7 Móstoles - Sevilla la Nueva, Camino Viejo de Navalcarnero, Camino de las Labores, and the Cañada Real Leonesa, among others.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should bring ample water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing for the weather. A GPS device or the Komoot app is highly recommended for navigation.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or water sources along the way?
faqs.answer: This is a largely natural and rural route, so dedicated facilities like restrooms or official water sources are scarce. It's crucial to carry enough water for the entire duration of your ride. You might find amenities in the towns of Sevilla la Nueva and Colmenar del Arroyo at the start or end of your journey.
